steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has a subject ('a team of researchers') and a main verb ('analyzed geotagged social media posts'). The blank comes between them and needs a word form that modifies the subject without creating a second main verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — a conjugated verb form: Creates a second main verb, making the sentence grammatically broken. ✗
  • Choice B — another conjugated form: Same problem — two competing main verbs with no coordinating conjunction. ✗
  • Choice C — another conjugated form: Same problem. ✗
  • Choice D — 'hoping': A present participle that modifies 'a team of researchers' and lets 'analyzed' remain the sole main verb. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
